title: ED altered mental status documentation

examination.text: Supports documenting recent hours-to-days changes or fluctuations in cognition, perception, physical function and social behaviour reported by the person or an informant.
examination.rationale: The exact NICE delirium indicators support documenting recent onset or fluctuation, associated cognitive, perceptual, physical-function or social-behaviour change, relevant negatives and clinician-assessed risk context in adults at risk; they do not cover every altered-mental-status cause.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting recent hours-to-days changes or fluctuations in cognition, perception, physical function and social behaviour reported by the person or an informant.

source.title: Delirium: prevention, diagnosis and management in hospital and long-term care

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.3.1 — risk factors and indicators at presentation

examination.text: Supports documenting observed changes and, when performed by a competent practitioner, the named structured assessment and clinician-confirmed diagnosis; no diagnosis is inferred by the template.
examination.rationale: The exact section supports documenting observed changes and a competent structured assessment only when actually performed; it does not auto-select an examination or diagnosis.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting observed changes and, when performed by a competent practitioner, the named structured assessment and clinician-confirmed diagnosis; no diagnosis is inferred by the template.

source.title: Delirium: prevention, diagnosis and management in hospital and long-term care

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.5.2–1.6.4 — document changes and competent assessment

sources_used.title: Delirium: prevention, diagnosis and management in hospital and long-term care
sources_used.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE legal and pathway adaptation

source_limitations: Pediatric presentations, Glasgow Coma Scale, collateral history details, glucose, intoxication, trauma, stroke, seizure, infection, metabolic causes, capacity, investigations, diagnosis, treatment, observation and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
